opportunities.name: Real-time Zero Trust Data and Access Control for Combat Systems
opportunities.objective: Develop a real-time Zero Trust data access control system for combat systems.
opportunities.notes: High alignment, but Hoplynk does not currently have Advanced Authentication (biometrics/MFA) or a blockchain-based immutable access-logging capability the Navy is exploring. Targets: cut access latency 50%+, cut unauthorized-access risk 90%+, cut admin overhead 25%+.

opportunities.name: Universal Automated Control Assessment, Validation & Risk Correlation Platform for Hybrid DoW Environments
opportunities.objective: Develop a universal assessment framework that automates Control Validation Tests (CVT) across Cloud, On-Premise, and Air-Gapped/Tactical environments, ingesting vulnerability scanner data and correlating findings to NIST SP 800-53 controls.
opportunities.notes: High alignment.

opportunities.name: Adaptive Sensor Management
opportunities.objective: Develop an algorithmic capability for dynamic resource allocation across Ship Self-Defense System (SSDS) sensor tasking, identifying sensors that can be released for other tasking without sacrificing track quality, and where reallocated resources would help most.
opportunities.notes: High alignment.

opportunities.name: High-Throughput Embarked Data Transfer
opportunities.objective: Develop a small form-factor device (one cubic foot stowed, including transceiver) and supporting software to enable high-throughput data package transmission off embarked Navy platforms.
opportunities.notes: High alignment, only caveat is the Navy is looking specifically at free-space laser/IR links rather than fiber or traditional RF. Target: 4TB in 60 seconds (~67GB/s sustained) over 5,000+ nautical miles.

opportunities.name: Secure Tasking of Commercial Assets
opportunities.objective: Develop a capability for intercommunication between government and commercial satellites, including end-to-end encryption within existing commercial tasking interfaces, secure data transfer protocols, and blockchain-based verification of tasking request authenticity/integrity.
opportunities.notes: Baseline focus is secure tasking at required CUI levels; later phases target a 90% reduction in tasking latency.

opportunities.name: Asymmetric Collaborative Counter Swarm
opportunities.objective: Develop and integrate distributed AI that collaboratively commands a multi-agent Group 1/2 UAS swarm to defend against a numerically superior enemy swarm, interfacing with existing platform autonomy/perception and without relying on a centralized control node.
opportunities.notes: Good fit as a teaming partner/subcontractor -- Hoplynk provides the connective/decentralized networking layer while a partner takes the swarm-autonomy tech.

opportunities.name: Anomalous Behavior Detection and Alerting for Congested Maritime Environments
opportunities.objective: Detect and alert on anomalous vessel/asset behavior in congested maritime environments.
opportunities.notes: Adjacent fit.

opportunities.name: T3CP Patent Holiday SBIR Open Topic Call
opportunities.objective: Open-topic SBIR call under the Patent Holiday program (no fixed technical topic).
opportunities.notes: Relevant -- open topic, worth reviewing against whichever Hoplynk product has the strongest narrative that cycle.

opportunities.name: Humanoid Robotic Fleet Management
opportunities.objective: Effectively and efficiently coordinate multiple humanoid and industrial robots for complex tasks in dynamic industrial environments.
opportunities.notes: Not yet scored for fit -- fleet coordination angle could map to Argus, but confirm before applying.

opportunities.name: DIU Presents: Specular MIST Prize Challenge -- LOE 3: Passive Electronic Surveillance
opportunities.objective: Provide remotely operable electronic surveillance capabilities at scale to crewed and uncrewed surface platforms; must sense/process the RF environment with remote operator input and provide state-of-the-art cybersecurity compatible with the existing Navy landing zone.
opportunities.notes: Might be worth partnering with someone else for the submission -- Hoplynk's edge is the connectivity/comms layer, not the RF sensing itself.

opportunities.name: Expeditionary Very Low Frequency (ExVLF) Transmitter System
opportunities.objective: Not yet detailed -- pulled from SAM.gov listing, needs a follow-up read of the full opportunity notice.
opportunities.notes: Expeditionary VLF transmitter -- possible fit for GoLynk's rapid-deploy angle. Needs manual review of the SAM.gov listing for dates/requirements.

opportunities.name: NTIA Public Wireless Supply Chain Innovation Fund
opportunities.objective: Federal funding supporting open, interoperable, standards-based wireless network equipment and technologies.
opportunities.notes: Standing program, not a dated solicitation -- check current funding rounds/NOFOs periodically.
